major_arc_linear_lower_bound.statement: for all even n > majorArcThreshold, coefficient * n + minorArcDftBound(n)^2 <= rawVonMangoldtFourierMajorArcContribution(majorArcs, n)

minor_arc_uniform_dft_bound_valid.statement: for all even n > minorArcThreshold and k outside majorArcs(n), |vonMangoldtZModDft(n,k)| <= minorArcDftBound(n)

contamination_dominated.statement: for all even n > contaminationThreshold, 2 * vonMangoldtWeightSumContaminationBudget(canonicalNonPrimePrimePowerVonMangoldtWeightSumBound, n) < coefficient * n

contamination_sqrt_log_model_bound.statement: for all even n > contaminationThreshold, vonMangoldtSqrtLogBudgetComparisonConstant * sqrt(n) * log(n)^3 < coefficient * n

notes: Template only. This is the fixed-square-error direct DFT positive-linear route: the analytic side supplies a lower bound for the real major-frequency contribution with the minor budget fixed to minorArcDftBound(n)^2, a uniform minor DFT estimate, and explicit domination of the canonical prime-power contamination budget. There is no separate minorArcError field and no minor_arc_dft_bound_sq_error_bound obligation. If contaminationThreshold >= 2, the optional contamination_sqrt_log_model_bound can derive contamination_dominated through the formalized sqrt(n)*log(n)^3 comparison theorem. Lean derives the final threshold from majorArcThreshold, minorArcThreshold, and contaminationThreshold.
